fields.label: Coats
fields.help: Number of paint layers to apply.

explanation:
**Painting projects** require technical calculations: coverage rates, surface conditions, paint type, and application method all affect your material needs. Understanding these variables helps you estimate accurately and avoid waste.

## How to Calculate

This calculator applies the standard formula: total liters = (area in m² × number of coats) ÷ coverage rate (m²/L). Input your values and it verifies ranges, alerting you to unusual estimates.

## Important Notes

Use this as a planning estimate. For critical decisions (professional projects, contract bids), consult a painting contractor. Data is current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What paint coverage rates should I use?
faq.a: Latex interior: 8-12 m²/L. Enamel: 10-14 m²/L. Waterproof/anti-humidity: 6-8 m²/L. Always check your specific paint can for exact coverage.

faq.q: How many coats of paint do I actually need?
faq.a: Typically 2 coats for interior walls. Porous surfaces absorb more on the first coat. Dark colors often require 3 coats for full opacity.

faq.q: Should I use a paint roller or brush?
faq.a: Rollers create 20% less waste and work faster for large areas. Use brushes for edges, corners, and detailed trim work.

faq.q: How many coats for exterior paint?
faq.a: Exterior painting requires 2-3 coats minimum for durability. Use exterior latex acrylic or synthetic enamel rated for weather.

faq.q: Why do dark paint colors need more coats?
faq.a: Dark colors have lower opacity and need an extra coat to achieve uniform, full coverage.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
These estimates use standard industry formulas. For specific projects, verify amounts with your contractor or paint manufacturer.

faq.q: Does this tool store my information?
faq.a: No. All calculations run in your browser. We don't send or store any data on servers.
